invalid_review_attempts.reason: Shell quoting failed before Claude prompt execution; no review result was produced and this attempt is not counted as a valid pack-level review.
invalid_review_attempts.command_shape: claude prompt passed as a shell argument with unescaped diff content

parsed_review.summary: Clean descriptor-only/no-write terminal closeout that follows the CP00-135..154 pattern. Counts (28), unit IDs (RP03.P09.M07.S13-RP03.P09.M10.S04), distributions, H03/C03 gates, CP00-154 inheritance, and CP00-156/RP04.P00.M00.S01 handoff triangulate correctly across contract, code, test, and validator. Schema bump v0.21->v0.22 and current_pack flip are consistent. No execution, evaluation, materialization, command rerun, audit/product writes, LDIP, or HRX split present; all catalog functions are pure and side-effect-free. Two non-blocking P3 observations.

findings.title: M10 phase reuses review-question template; catalog title/deliverable_type metadata is not cross-validated against the plan
findings.evidence: review-terminal-closeout-catalog.js CP155_MICRO_PHASES: micro M10 'Closeout And Next Handoff' uses template: REVIEW_PACKET_UNITS with count:4, so its 4 units inherit review-question titles (Architecture/Security/Permission bypass/Audit completeness review questions) that do not match the closeout/handoff phase role; the pack's last unit RP03.P09.M10.S04 is thus labeled 'Audit completeness questions'. validateAuditComplianceCp155Coverage() only checks unit ID count/order/uniqueness against planPack.included_units[].id; titles, deliverable_type, and evidence_mode are self-defined by the templates and not reconciled with the plan. The deliverable/evidence-mode distribution asserts (implementation:11, claude_review:7, security_audit:6, test:2, ui:2; 8/8/8/4) are tautological with the templates.
findings.recommended_action: Confirm M10.S01-S04 catalog titles/deliverable_types against closeout-pack-plan.json (or relabel M10 units to closeout/handoff-specific titles). Optionally extend coverage validation to reconcile catalog title/deliverable_type with the plan so distribution assertions are plan-backed rather than self-referential. Non-blocking; coverage is ID-based and the boundary is unaffected.

findings.title: No-write boundary is declarative (hardcoded attestation flags), enforced by code inspection rather than runtime
findings.evidence: freezeNoWriteResult() and CP155_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION hardcode ~70 *_false flags; audit.test.js and validate-rp03-audit-architecture.mjs assert those same flags are false. The guarantee that no audit/product writes, network, UI, or command execution occur rests on the catalog containing no fs/network/db/exec calls (verified: every exported function is a pure transform over frozen constants, incl. createAuditComplianceCp155HermesEvidencePacket which only labels supplied commands status:'passed' without running them).
findings.recommended_action: No change required; flagged for transparency. The declarative flags provide regression protection (a future real implementation flipping a flag would fail tests) and contract<->code sync, which is the appropriate assurance level for a Risk C descriptor pack.

parsed_review.p3_disposition: two non-blocking descriptor-scope residual risks accepted/deferred; no code changes required for pack closeout
